label: Mobile Forensics Analyst
description: Extracts and analyzes data from mobile devices. Covers acquisition methodologies, application artifact analysis, encrypted device handling, and cloud account data.

paths.name: Vendor Tool Track (Cellebrite)
paths.rationale: Cellebrite dominates the mobile forensics tool market — particularly in law enforcement. UFED-based certifications are required by name in most government and LE mobile forensics positions.
pros: Cellebrite certs required by name at most LE mobile forensics positions
UFED is the dominant tool in government and law enforcement
Widely recognized in courts as practitioner qualification
cons: Platform-specific — limited value outside Cellebrite environments
Tool-dependent certification becomes outdated as tools evolve

knowledge: Evidence handling and chain of custody
prerequisites.experience: Law enforcement or investigation background for LE track. Cellebrite UFED access required for exam.

cert_sequence.notes: Cellebrite Certified Operator — entry-level covering UFED operation, physical and logical acquisition. Required before CCPA. Register through Cellebrite's training portal.

Cellebrite Certified Physical Analyst — advanced analysis of UFED extraction data, file system structures, deleted data recovery, and app artifact analysis. Primary Cellebrite credential for practicing mobile forensics examiners.
Cellebrite Certified Mobile Examiner — complex extractions, encrypted devices, cloud data acquisition, specialized app analysis. Capstone Cellebrite certification for senior mobile forensics roles.

paths.name: Vendor-Neutral Track
paths.rationale: GASF is the recognized vendor-neutral mobile forensics standard — covering both Android and iOS methodology without tool vendor lock-in.
pros: GASF is vendor-neutral and DoD approved
GIME adds mobile malware and app analysis depth
cons: Less recognized than Cellebrite certs in LE environments where UFED is the standard tool

cert_sequence.notes: GIAC Advanced Smartphone Forensics — iOS and Android forensics including physical, logical, and filesystem acquisition, app artifact analysis, cloud data forensics. DoD 8140 approved. Open-book — build comprehensive notes. Associated with SANS FOR585.
GIAC iOS and macOS Examiner — specialized iOS forensics including newer iOS security features, iPhone file system analysis, and macOS mobile integration. Extends GASF iOS coverage significantly.
